sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, data XML, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, description) VALUES (1, '<root><item>Sample</item></root>', 'Testing XML and varchar interactions');
SELECT dbname(), xpath_contains(data, '/root/item') FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT dbname() AS current_db, COUNT(*) FROM test_table GROUP BY dbname();
SELECT dbname() AS current_db, id FROM test_table WHERE xpath_contains(data, '/root/item') AND dbname() = 'Demo';
CREATE TABLE another_table (key_id INT, value INT);
INSERT INTO another_table (key_id, value) VALUES (1, 100), (2, NULL), (3, 250);
SELECT a.id, dbname(), COALESCE(b.value, 0) FROM test_table a LEFT JOIN another_table b ON a.id = b.key_id WHERE xpath_contains(a.data, '/root/item');

SELECT dbname(), SUM(COALESCE(b.value, 0)) OVER (PARTITION BY a.id) FROM test_table a LEFT JOIN another_table b ON a.id = b.key_id WHERE a.id IS NOT NULL AND xpath_contains(a.data, '/root/item') GROUP BY dbname();
SELECT dbname(), MAX(CASE WHEN xpath_contains(data, '/root/item') THEN id END) AS max_id FROM test_table HAVING MAX(id) IS NOT NULL;
